perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: All Sensitive Data disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original text consistently uses 'Confidential Information'. The modified text replaces it with 'Sensitive Data' in this section. 'Sensitive Data' isn't defined, and it is unclear whether it is meant to be the same as 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1. This creates confusion about which types of data are subject to this specific restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Sensitive Data disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient agrees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidentia l Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a reasonable degree o f care. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Re cipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text consistently uses 'Confidential Information'. The modified text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data'. The explanation notes that 'Sensitive Data' isn't defined and it is unclear whether it is meant to be the same as 'Confidential Information' which is defined in Section 1. The contradicted text then refers to both 'Sensitive Data' and 'Confidential Information', implying that they are distinct. This creates ambiguity and potential contradiction, because obligations and restrictions depend on whether certain data is one or the other, or both. This ambiguity about which data is subject to specific restrictions creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain with Disclosing Par ty or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: All right title and interest in and to the Protected Information shall remain with Disclosing Par ty or its licensors.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'Confidential Information'. The modified text changes it to 'Protected Information'. 'Protected Information' isn't defined, leading to uncertainty about what rights the Disclosing Party retains and potentially conflicting interpretations of ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right title and interest in and to the Protected Information shall remain with Disclosing Par ty or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WAR RANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON -INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information,' which is referenced in the contradicted text ('ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS"...'). The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information,' which is already defined and has different rights and responsibilities associated with it. This substitution creates a direct contradiction because the warranty disclaimer now applies to 'Protected Information' instead of 'Confidential Information,' potentially altering the Disclosing Party's liability and warranty obligations regarding that information.
